verdict: The occupation of Public Defender is at high risk of AI displacement, with a score of 26 indicating that 74% of tasks are likely to be automated by current or near-future AI capabilities.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine_cognitive score
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like interpreting laws, analyzing probable outcomes of cases, and preparing legal documents are highly routine-cognitively demanding and can be easily automated by current or near-future AI capabilities.

reasoning_steps.title: Low creative_judgment score
reasoning_steps.body: While some creativity is required in public defense, the tasks involved are not as novel or original as those in other professions, making them less susceptible to AI displacement.

adjacent_careers.name: Lawyer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: While some tasks may be automated, the creative judgment and relationship aspects of being a lawyer are still essential and less susceptible to AI displacement.

adjacent_careers.name: Legal Assistant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Tasks like preparing legal documents and conducting research can be automated, but the high-stakes nature of public defense requires human judgment and decision-making.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered legal document preparation tool launched
recent_developments.impact: This new tool may automate some tasks, but it is not yet capable of replacing human judgment and decision-making in complex cases.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.
